title: Brew'n Chew
description: A potioned way to eat your food!
body:
###

Potion Infused Food

Have you ever wanted to mix any food item with any potion effect!?

Even the witch villagers told me this was a bad idea. But who listens to them anyway?


| **Brewing Cauldron** | **Mixing Zone** | |:-------------------------------------:|:------------------------------:| | Craft this **special block** featuring **four tabs** and **dynamic visuals** that change based on its contents. | First tab for combining **any food** with **potions** to create **infused food items** with **unique effects**. | | **Flower Infusion** | **Sound Control** | | Second tab for customizing **tooltip colors**, a chance of discovering a new version of a potion effect, and toggling **enchantment visuals** on your infused foods. | Third tab to toggle the cauldron's **bubbling sound effects** for **distraction-free** brewing. | ###

Floriography

The fourth tab lets you open a book and check the infusion combinations you’ve unlocked.


###

Potion Balancing

Find 4 potion items (of any kind) and choose one food item to start experimenting!

Each potion will halve its duration to properly balance the mechanic.

When using the same potion type with different amplifiers, the output takes the highest amplifier while reducing the duration of the lower one by 8 times + 4 for each amplifier difference.


###

Flower Infusion

In the second tab, set up an infused flower to change the tooltip of your food item.

Different vanilla flowers and mushrooms provide unique visual modification styles.

Preview your customization with the example steak renderer (not pickable).

###

Floriography

In the fourth tab, you’ll find a set of locked flowers, displayed with their cryptic silhouettes.

Once unlocked, each flower reveals a semi realistic drawing, a brief description and the bonus effect it grants to your infused food.

Combine the right potion with a flower to unlock it and reveal its bonus.

###

Flower & Mushroom Styles

Warning: Spoiler alert! Don’t click the button below if you’d like to discover each effect on your own.

Spoiler - **Dandelion:** Jump effect +1 level - **Poppy:** Strength boost: +20% damage vs. targets below 33% health - **Blue Orchid:** Adds Protection II to Slowness - **Allium:** Absorption starts weaker but regenerates extra hearts over time - **Azure Bluet:** 5% chance Haste triggers Speed for 5 s - **Red Tulip:** Stronger Harm effect + adds Luck - **Orange Tulip:** Fire Resistance +1 minute - **White Tulip:** Removes slow fall motion but still prevents fall damage - **Pink Tulip:** 3% chance Regeneration refills hunger - **Oxeye Daisy:** Speed +20% on snow - **Cornflower:** 5% chance Water Breathing grants Dolphin’s Grace for 10 s - **Lily of the Valley:** Poison effect spreads to nearby enemies within 3 blocks - **Red Mushroom:** Adds enchanting effect - **Brown Mushroom:** Removes enchanting effect - **Wither Rose:** Resets item to vanilla
